name: Antas da Masmorra — circular route (PR8 TVR)
name_original: Antas da Masmorra — percurso circular (PR8 TVR)
region_intro: Jogging around Cachopo offers a network of routes primarily characterized by hilly terrain and paths winding through rural landscapes. The region features varied elevation changes, with some routes ascending over 500 meters, indicating a challenging environment for runners. Many trails are circular, providing diverse experiences through the local countryside. The area's natural features include paths through areas that may feature almond trees and connections to the Serra do Caldeirão mountain range. Best jogging routes around Cachopo The most popular jogging route is Morenos — circular route (PR13 TVR), a 8.8 miles (14.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 37 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain, making it suitable for experienced runners. Another top favourite among local runners is Fonte de Águas de Tábuas Trail (PR14 TVR), a difficult 5.8 miles (9.4 km) path. This route includes over 200 meters of ascent, offering a challenging run through the landscape. Local runners also love the Almond Tree Path (PR8), a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 22 minutes. Jogging around Cachopo is defined by hilly paths, rural landscapes, and routes connecting to the Serra do Caldeirão. The network offers options for different ability levels, including moderate and difficult running routes. The routes in Cachopo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars. More than 40 runners have used komoot to explore Cachopo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Cachopo?
A: There are 10 dedicated running routes around Cachopo, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These trails are highly rated by the komoot community.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Cachopo?
A: The jogging trails around Cachopo are primarily characterized by hilly terrain and paths winding through rural landscapes. You can expect varied elevation changes, with some routes ascending over 500 meters, making for a challenging run. Many paths connect to the Serra do Caldeirão mountain range.

Q: Are there any easy running routes for beginners in Cachopo?
A: While many routes in Cachopo are rated as difficult, there are a few moderate options suitable for those looking for a less strenuous run. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1418051633/almond-tree-path-pr8" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almond Tree Path (PR8)&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 11.1 km trail that can be completed in about 1 hour 22 minutes.

Q: What are some of the longer running routes in the Cachopo area?
A: For a longer challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1422466836/between-vales-fontes-and-memorias-da-serra-do-caldeirao-circular-route-pr2-sba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Between Vales Fontes and Memórias da Serra do Caldeirão — circular route (PR2 SBA)&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 18.3 km with over 500 meters of ascent. Another substantial option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1422319554/morenos-circular-route-pr13-tvr" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morenos — circular route (PR13 TVR)&lt;/a&gt;, a 14.2 km trail.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Cachopo?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Cachopo are circular, offering diverse experiences without retracing your steps. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1422319554/morenos-circular-route-pr13-tvr" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morenos — circular route (PR13 TVR)&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1422367691/antas-da-masmorra-circular-route-pr8-tvr" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Antas da Masmorra — circular route (PR8 TVR)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What interesting sights or landmarks can I see while running in Cachopo?
A: While running in Cachopo, you might encounter various points of interest. The area features natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4048452" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tábuas Summit&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7103577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cork Oak with Harvest Year Marking&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3423560" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa dos Cantoneiros de Barranco do Velho&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4810360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova dos Mouros Mining Park&lt;/a&gt;. Don't miss the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6277196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto da Ameixeira Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; for scenic views.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Cachopo?
A: The running routes in Cachopo are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 5.0 stars. Over 40 runners have explored these trails using komoot, often praising the varied terrain and the challenging yet rewarding experiences offered by the hilly, rural landscapes.

Q: Are there any trails that pass through almond groves?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1418051633/almond-tree-path-pr8" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almond Tree Path (PR8)&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to lead through areas that may feature almond trees, offering a picturesque running experience through local flora.

Q: Are there any places to eat or rest near the running routes in Cachopo?
A: While specific amenities on every route aren't detailed, the area includes settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/168371" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vaqueiros&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/389982" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco do Velho&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5522913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tia Bia Restaurant&lt;/a&gt;, which could be a convenient stop for refreshments.

Q: What is the most challenging running route in Cachopo?
A: One of the most challenging routes is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1422466836/between-vales-fontes-and-memorias-da-serra-do-caldeirao-circular-route-pr2-sba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Between Vales Fontes and Memórias da Serra do Caldeirão — circular route (PR2 SBA)&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult 18.3 km trail features a significant elevation gain of over 520 meters, making it suitable for experienced runners seeking a demanding workout.

Q: Can I find trails that offer views of the Serra do Caldeirão?
A: Many of the routes in Cachopo connect to or pass through the Serra do Caldeirão mountain range, offering opportunities for scenic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1422466836/between-vales-fontes-and-memorias-da-serra-do-caldeirao-circular-route-pr2-sba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Between Vales Fontes and Memórias da Serra do Caldeirão — circular route (PR2 SBA)&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, designed to explore this mountainous region.
tips: Mealha is a small village in Tavira. Its main attraction is the rustic water fountain, located in the central part of the place, which still serves the local population. From here, several trails leave for the region.

Anta da Masmorra, also known as Anta do Cerro da Masmorra, is a megalithic monument located in the parish of Cachopo. It was studied in the 1970s, and the structure indicates that it dates from the late Neolithic or Chalcolithic. It is associated with other archaeological sites in the region, such as Dungeon 2 and Dungeon 3.
